verses.text: Clean beasts both part the hoof and chew the cud. Clean beasts. God marks what may be eaten.
Clean beasts both part the hoof and chew the cud. Clean beasts. God marks what may be eaten.
Clean beasts both part the hoof and chew the cud. Clean beasts. God marks what may be eaten.
The camel, coney, hare, and swine are unclean. Unclean. Some that seem fit are forbidden.
The camel, coney, hare, and swine are unclean. Unclean. Some that seem fit are forbidden.
The camel, coney, hare, and swine are unclean. Unclean. Some that seem fit are forbidden.
The camel, coney, hare, and swine are unclean. Unclean. Some that seem fit are forbidden.
The camel, coney, hare, and swine are unclean. Unclean. Some that seem fit are forbidden.
Water creatures with fins and scales are clean; the rest an abomination. Of the waters. Only the marked may be eaten.
Water creatures with fins and scales are clean; the rest an abomination. Of the waters. Only the marked may be eaten.
Water creatures with fins and scales are clean; the rest an abomination. Of the waters. Only the marked may be eaten.
Water creatures with fins and scales are clean; the rest an abomination. Of the waters. Only the marked may be eaten.
The unclean birds are listed — eagle, vulture, and the rest. Unclean birds. Birds of prey are forbidden.
The unclean birds are listed — eagle, vulture, and the rest. Unclean birds. Birds of prey are forbidden.
The unclean birds are listed — eagle, vulture, and the rest. Unclean birds. Birds of prey are forbidden.
The unclean birds are listed — eagle, vulture, and the rest. Unclean birds. Birds of prey are forbidden.
The owl, stork, and bat are unclean. Night birds. The unclean list is completed.
The owl, stork, and bat are unclean. Night birds. The unclean list is completed.
The owl, stork, and bat are unclean. Night birds. The unclean list is completed.
Flying insects are unclean, save those that leap. Insects. A few are allowed for food.
Flying insects are unclean, save those that leap. Insects. A few are allowed for food.
Flying insects are unclean, save those that leap. Insects. A few are allowed for food.
Flying insects are unclean, save those that leap. Insects. A few are allowed for food.
Touching their carcases makes one unclean till evening. Defiled. Death pollutes the living.
Touching their carcases makes one unclean till evening. Defiled. Death pollutes the living.
Touching their carcases makes one unclean till evening. Defiled. Death pollutes the living.
Touching their carcases makes one unclean till evening. Defiled. Death pollutes the living.
Touching their carcases makes one unclean till evening. Defiled. Death pollutes the living.
Creeping things — the weasel, mouse, and lizards — are unclean. Creepers. Small creatures too defile.
Creeping things — the weasel, mouse, and lizards — are unclean. Creepers. Small creatures too defile.
Creeping things — the weasel, mouse, and lizards — are unclean. Creepers. Small creatures too defile.
Whatever they fall on is unclean and must be cleansed or broken. Spreading defilement. Uncleanness touches all it reaches.
Whatever they fall on is unclean and must be cleansed or broken. Spreading defilement. Uncleanness touches all it reaches.
Whatever they fall on is unclean and must be cleansed or broken. Spreading defilement. Uncleanness touches all it reaches.
Whatever they fall on is unclean and must be cleansed or broken. Spreading defilement. Uncleanness touches all it reaches.
Springs stay clean, but wetted seed defiles. Limits. God sets the bounds of defilement.
Springs stay clean, but wetted seed defiles. Limits. God sets the bounds of defilement.
Springs stay clean, but wetted seed defiles. Limits. God sets the bounds of defilement.
A clean beast that dies of itself makes the eater unclean. Carrion. Even clean flesh, found dead, defiles.
A clean beast that dies of itself makes the eater unclean. Carrion. Even clean flesh, found dead, defiles.
Creeping things are not to be eaten; they are an abomination. Not to eat. Defiling food is shunned.
Creeping things are not to be eaten; they are an abomination. Not to eat. Defiling food is shunned.
Creeping things are not to be eaten; they are an abomination. Not to eat. Defiling food is shunned.
'Be ye holy; for I am holy'; discern between clean and unclean. Be holy. God's people are set apart by holiness.
'Be ye holy; for I am holy'; discern between clean and unclean. Be holy. God's people are set apart by holiness.
'Be ye holy; for I am holy'; discern between clean and unclean. Be holy. God's people are set apart by holiness.
